title: In-pi read-schema op returns the projected name-list, not the full subtree (needs whole:true at ops-registry.ts:1166)
description: Distinct from FGAP-020's CLI-surface correction: the IN-PI read-schema op itself returns the paged `required` name-list for an addressed object node, because it wraps addr.value in structureForRead (ops-registry.ts:1166) which pages on the node's incidental array (read-element.ts:266-285). A CLI-only fix leaves in-pi agents still receiving the projection. Correcting BOTH surfaces requires passing whole:true at ops-registry.ts:1166 — a deliberate change to in-pi op behavior, which the parity survey's frozen-ops constraint explicitly carved out and the user has now authorized as a tracked gap.

evidence.reference: read-schema op wraps the addressed node in structureForRead WITHOUT whole:true — the in-pi op projection point; whole:true here forces full-subtree serialization while retaining the 50KB cap
whole:true forces whole-object serialization and skips collection discovery
the orchestrator script already returns the full subtree (JSON.stringify(addr.value)) — the behavior the in-pi op should match
the GAP-2 residual / REQUIRES-OP-CHANGE branch in §D/§E of the parity survey

impact: In-pi agents (not only the CLI) reading an object schema node via read-schema --path receive the field-name list, not the schema subtree — the same defect class as FGAP-020 but on the in-pi surface, which the CLI-side fix does not reach.
proposed_resolution: Pass whole:true into structureForRead at ops-registry.ts:1166 for the addressed-node case so the in-pi op returns the full subtree (matching scripts/orchestrator/read-schema.ts:181), while retaining the 50KB cap. This deliberately changes in-pi op behavior (user-authorized). Re-confirm via the canonical pipeline; class-check other read ops routing addressed object nodes through structureForRead.
